title: Jack Hannaford | Part 08
fullText: The farmer looked at the sky, but he could not see the amazing rainbow or the man walking on it. “Here,” said the soldier, “kneel down by the side of the road and look up at the sky like I’m doing.” “I will if you’ll hold my horse,” said the farmer, and he jumped down from his horse. As soon as the farmer knelt down, Jack Hannaford jumped onto the horse and escaped! Now, who do you think was more foolish, the farmer or his wife?

sentences.en: The farmer looked at the sky,
sentences.pt: O fazendeiro olhou para o céu,
sentences.phonetic: dâ FÁR-mer LÚKT ét dâ SKÁI

sentences.en: but he could not see the amazing rainbow
sentences.pt: mas ele não conseguiu ver o incrível arco-íris
sentences.phonetic: bât rri cud nót SÍ dâ â-MÊI-zing RÊIN-bou

sentences.en: or the man walking on it.
sentences.pt: ou o homem andando sobre ele.
sentences.phonetic: ór dâ MÉN UÓ-king ón it

sentences.en: “kneel down by the side of the road”

sentences.en: “and look up at the sky like I’m doing.”
sentences.pt: “e olhe para o céu como eu estou fazendo.”
sentences.phonetic: énd LÚK ÂP ét dâ SKÁI láik áim DÚ-ing

sentences.pt: “Eu vou se você segurar meu cavalo”, disse o fazendeiro,
sentences.phonetic: ái UÍL if iúl RRÔULD mái RRÓRS, séd dâ FÁR-mer

sentences.en: and he jumped down from his horse.
sentences.pt: e ele pulou do seu cavalo.
sentences.phonetic: énd rri DJÂMPT DÁUN from rriz RRÓRS

sentences.en: As soon as the farmer knelt down,
sentences.phonetic: éz SÚN éz dâ FÁR-mer NÉLT DÁUN

sentences.en: Now, who do you think was more foolish, the farmer or his wife?
sentences.pt: Agora, quem você acha que foi mais tolo, o fazendeiro ou sua esposa?
sentences.phonetic: NÁU, RRÚ du iú THÍNK uóz MÓR FÚ-lish, dâ FÁR-mer ór rriz UÁIF

vocabulary.en: by the side of the road
vocabulary.phonetic: bai dâ SÁID óv dâ RÔUD

vocabulary.pt: pulou para baixo / desceu de um pulo

grammarNotes.title: As soon as
grammarNotes.note: Conector de tempo = “logo que / assim que”. Liga duas ações em sequência imediata: As soon as the farmer knelt down, Jack jumped onto the horse.

grammarNotes.title: Kneel → knelt (verbo irregular)
grammarNotes.note: kneel / knelt / knelt. O “k” inicial é mudo: NEEL, NELT.

grammarNotes.title: Contração you’ll
grammarNotes.note: you’ll = you will. “I will if you’ll hold my horse” — aqui o will aparece na condicional (if) porque expressa disposição/promessa, não apenas futuro.

grammarNotes.title: Phrasal verbs com down/up/onto
grammarNotes.note: kneel down (ajoelhar), look up (olhar para cima), jump down (descer de um pulo), jump onto (pular em cima de). A preposítion muda o sentido do movimento.
